home.hero_sub: Python library for weather, market, and harvest data in Benin and West Africa.
home.modules_sub: Weather data, agricultural markets and data pipelines — built for African field conditions.
home.market_desc: Economic analysis of agricultural markets. Price forecasting, spatial arbitrage, real transport costs and decision support.
home.weather_desc: Historical and forecast climate data. Phenology, FAO-56 water balance, drought risk indicators.
home.kidas_desc: Agricultural data processing pipeline. Cleaning, validation, normalization with FAO codes and local units.
home.for_who_sub: KadiPy addresses the real needs of African agriculture professionals.
home.profile_dev_desc: Integrate reliable agricultural data into your applications without rebuilding pipelines. Intuitive API, offline cache, silent fallback.
home.profile_agro_desc: Compute GDD, estimate optimal sowing dates, and anticipate water deficits without internet access.
home.profile_researcher_desc: Access CHIRPS, ERA5 and WFP data in a few lines. Normalize and validate your datasets with tools specific to West Africa.
home.profile_advisor_desc: Recommend: sell now or store? Transport to Cotonou or Malanville? With an integrated confidence score.
home.code_example_sub: Spatial arbitrage Parakou → Cotonou for 10 tons of maize, with weather integration.
home.map_sub: 9 geolocated markets and 3 agro-ecological zones in Benin.
home.before_1: Manually scraping WFP prices to CSV
home.before_2: Writing your own GPS routing logic
home.cta_sub: One command to access African agricultural data.

why.title: Why KadiPy exists
why.problem_desc: Agricultural data tools available in 2024 do not match African field realities. Data is scattered across incompatible APIs, internet connections are unreliable in rural areas, and every project starts from scratch.
why.solution_desc: KadiPy unifies three essential data types (weather, market prices, harvests) into a single, coherent, offline-first API. Inspired by pandas ergonomics, it is designed to be learned in under an hour.
why.offline_desc: All data is cached locally (SQLite for weather, JSON for prices). The module works without any connection.
why.fallback_desc: If an API is unavailable, the module automatically switches to cached data or backup data without raising an error.
why.benin_desc: Local units (tiya, sac), geolocated markets (Dantokpa, Parakou, Malanville...), FAO crop codes, agro-ecological zones.

start.title: Quick Start
start.sub: Up and running in less than 5 minutes.
start.config_desc: Create a .env file at the root of your project to enable real WFP API data.
start.first_example_sub: Weather data + arbitrage recommendation in 12 lines.
start.faq_token_q: Is the WFP API key mandatory?
start.faq_token_a: No. Without a key, the module silently uses a local fallback price dictionary. The key is only needed for complete historical data.
start.faq_offline_q: Does it work without an internet connection?
start.faq_offline_a: Yes. After an initial online call, all data is cached locally. Subsequent calls automatically use the cache.
start.faq_python_a: Python 3.9 or higher. Tested on 3.9, 3.10, 3.11 and 3.12.

market.title: kadi.market
market.subtitle: Economic analysis of Beninese agricultural markets
market.desc: The market module models Beninese agricultural markets to help farmers, groups and advisors make better decisions: sell now or store, here or elsewhere.
market.example1_context: A farmer in Parakou wants to know if transporting 10 tons of maize to Cotonou is profitable.
market.example2_context: An advisor evaluates whether storing 5 tons of cowpea in Parakou for 2 months is more profitable than selling now.
market.example3_context: Impact of rain probability on logistics cost and confidence score.

weather.title: kadi.weather
weather.subtitle: Weather data and agronomic indicators
weather.desc: The weather module provides a unified interface to access historical and forecast climate data, and transform them into directly useful indicators for Beninese agriculture.
weather.example1_context: Detecting the start of the rainy season in Parakou to guide the maize sowing date.
weather.example2_context: Computing the FAO-56 water deficit for a cotton crop in Natitingou.
weather.example3_context: Rain probability to decide if transporting tomatoes to Cotonou is risky.

kidas.title: kadi.kidas
kidas.subtitle: Agricultural data processing pipeline
kidas.desc: kidas is the central data processing module in KadiPy. It handles loading, cleaning, validation, normalization and caching of data from files or REST APIs.

examples.title: Integrated examples
examples.subtitle: Real-world scenarios combining multiple KadiPy modules.
examples.example1_context: Survey data cleaning, weather check, sell/store recommendation for 20 tons of maize.
examples.example2_context: Maize GDD calculation, FAO water balance, SPI drought risk assessment over 3 months.
examples.example3_context: Automated CHIRPS NetCDF processing pipeline, WFP price enrichment, confidence score.
examples.example4_context: 3 sale scenarios (local, Cotonou, Malanville) for 5 tons of cowpea with costs and 30-day forecasts.

evolutions.title: Future evolutions
evolutions.subtitle: KadiPy project roadmap and timeline.
evolutions.contribute_desc: KadiPy is an open source project. All contributions are welcome: bug reports, suggestions, documentation fixes, new features.
